commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Remove -lbnetapi from Makefile
- Update README.md links
- Remove net API docs from memory
- The program doesn't use any network functionality
- Keep -ltracker for BFilePanel

commit.message:
Add component diagram in draw.io format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game hang after screensaver by delivering APU frame counter IRQs to CPU

The APU's isIrqPending() was never checked in the main loop, so games
relying on APU IRQs for timing (e.g. Double Dragon & Battletoads) would
spin indefinitely waiting for an interrupt that never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add APU implementation for sound support

Implement NES Audio Processing Unit with pulse, triangle, and noise
channels, wired into the CPU bus and main emulation loop. Audio output
uses javax.sound.sampled at 44100 Hz.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix game freeze after title screen by adding NMI edge detection, PRG RAM, and DMA NMI handling

- Trigger NMI immediately when game enables NMI via PPUCTRL while VBlank is active
- Add 8KB PRG RAM at $6000-$7FFF so game state reads/writes work correctly
- Check for NMI during DMA transfer to avoid missing VBlank interrupts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Printable/instant downloads unchanged — these are the volume sellers
- Updated prices on product pages, related sections, and bundle cards

- Saved stripe-price-updates.txt with all Stripe links needing updates

feat: Add Test Strategy tab to condition.html

- Universal 4-Test Minimum framework: one test per boundary position
  (loop entry, loop exit, index math, return check)
- Per-category minimal test suites for: Binary Search, Two Pointers,
  Sliding Window, Graph (BFS/DFS/Backtrack), DP, Intervals, Stack, Heap
- Each test: exact input → expected output → which bug it exposes → fix verify
- Decision table: 'which test to write first' by loop type
- Symptom table: 'what wrong answer looks like' for each bug type
- Core principle: a good boundary test FAILS against buggy code, PASSES correct

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Fix AnyTone DMR channel types, PMR power, and talk group export

Channel type was always 'D-Digital' regardless of mode; it should be 'DMR'
for pure-DMR repeaters and 'D+A TX D' when a repeater supports both FM and
DMR. Analog bandwidth was incorrectly set to 25K (should be 12.5K). TX
Permit was 'Always' instead of 'Off'. Analog channels lacked a default
contact ('Local' / ID 1). Color code now defaults to 1 instead of silently
dropping DMR channels with no color code set. PMR channels now correctly use
Low power as required by the EU PMR446 licence-free spec. Private-call
service IDs (Parrot, APRS, SMS Test, Repeater Info) are now always included
in TalkGroups.CSV as Private Call entries rather than being absent entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refactor ai-responder.yml to eliminate JSCPD duplication

Combined ai-respond-issue and ai-respond-pr jobs into a single ai-respond job
to fix JSCPD lint failures across all repos (26.14% duplication → 0%).

- Merged duplicate setup steps into one job
- Used conditional expressions for event-specific env vars
- Reduced file from 89 lines to 53 lines (-36 lines)

This fixes lint failures in 100+ repos caused by distributed workflow.

fix: use category lookup map instead of broken PostgREST self-join

PostgREST cannot resolve self-referential FK joins correctly — the
nested `parent:categories(id, name)` follows the FK in reverse,
returning an empty array instead of the parent object. Replace with
a separate category lookup query to resolve parent names reliably.

commit.message:
Fix YouTube quality and simplify Instagram format selection

- Remove youtube:player_client=android,ios — those clients now require
  GVS PO Tokens and get skipped, falling back to 360p only. Without
  specifying a client, yt-dlp auto-selects android_vr which gets 720p+
  formats without tokens.
- Simplify Instagram/TikTok/FB format selector: confirmed they use DASH
  streams (separate video+audio), so use bestvideo+bestaudio directly
  instead of the dead combined-stream branches.

commit.message:
fix(observability): search http.url.path for path filter in request log queries (#481)

The path filter was only searching the http.route tag, which contains Spring
MVC route templates (e.g., /api/{collectionName}/{id}). When navigating from
the performance page with a concrete path like /api/products/123, the wildcard
query never matched. Now searches both http.url.path (actual path) and
http.route (template) using an OR condition.

Co-authored-by: Craig Klinker <craigklinker@velocityglobal.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add comprehensive CLI tool for Agent Todo platform

Implemented a full-featured CLI tool with cobra framework:

Features:
- Authentication commands (login, register, logout, whoami)
- Project management (CRUD operations with filtering)
- Task management (CRUD, assignment, comments, status updates)
- Agent management (CRUD operations with enable/disable)

Configuration:
- YAML-based config stored in ~/.agent-todo/config.yaml
- Environment variable support
- Command-line flag overrides
- JWT token and API key management

Build System:
- Makefile with build, test, and completion targets
- Multi-platform build support (Linux, macOS, Windows)
- Shell completion for bash, zsh, fish, PowerShell

Documentation:
- Comprehensive 26KB README with all commands and examples
- Quick start guide and workflow examples
- Troubleshooting section and best practices

Access Control:
- Human users: JWT auth, can update any task
- AI agents: API key auth, can only update assigned tasks
- Separate endpoints for user vs agent operations

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remove Nitro IAP auto-init; make all IAP calls fully lazy

The startup crash (SIGABRT ~0.7s, same native UUIDs across builds) was
NitroIap auto-registering its Nitro JSI bridge before JS was ready.
JS-level try/catch cannot intercept crashes that happen before the JS
runtime touches any code.

Changes:
- app.json: remove "react-native-iap" Expo plugin (stops native IAP setup at startup)
- package.json: remove react-native-nitro-modules (removes Nitro JS runtime)
- lib/IApservice.ts: full rewrite — singleton gone, named async exports,
  react-native-iap loaded via dynamic import() only inside function bodies;
  nothing touches the IAP native layer at module parse time
- screens/FolderListScreen.js: remove static IAPService import, use named import
- screens/ProfileScreen.js: remove static IAPService import, remove
  initializeIAP() from useEffect, products fetched lazily on button tap
- screens/SubscriptionScreen.js: remove static IAPService import, remove
  fetchProducts useEffect, products + purchase happen entirely inside
  handleContinue (button tap only)
